pages.meta_description: My Background I was born in Bedford and raised and educated locally before attending Oxford University, where I earned my BA and I later completed an MBA at Harvard Business School. Real World Experience before becoming an MP

h3: Richard Fuller MP for North Bedfordshire
h2: Footer
pages.text_excerpt: About Richard | Richard Fuller MP Skip to main content Main navigation The Economy About News Campaigns Surgeries Policy and Campaign Responses Contact About Richard Tweet My Background I was born in Bedford and raised and educated locally before attending Oxford University, where I earned my BA and I later completed an MBA at Harvard Business School. Real World Experience before becoming an MP Before entering Parliament, I was a partner at the strategy consulting firm LEK Consulting and a founding partner at Investcorp's venture capital group. My business career includes helping start numerous small businesses and working in Australia, the United States, the Philippines, and South Korea. Between 2017 and 2019, I chaired an educational technology company. My passion for creativity, innovation, and the potential of start-ups and small businesses drives my commitment to putting this broad experience to work in Parliament. My Roles in Politics I served as the Member of Parliament for Bedford from 2010 to 2017 and was elected as the MP for North East Bedfordshire in December 2019 and re-elected as the MP for North Bedfordshire in 2024. I have been a member of the Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy Select Committee and, briefly, the Public Accounts Committee. I served as Economic Secretary to the Treasury from July to October 2022 and from July to October 2024 I was the Interim Chairman of the Conservative Party. I am currently the Shadow Chief Secretary to the Treasury. An Accessible Member of Parliament I take the concerns of local residents seriously, and I believe it is essential to understand your views to provide effective representation in Parliament. In the December 2019- May 2024 Parliament, I held 220 in-person and online/telephone surgeries, averaging four per month, along with 24 Town Halls since Covid restrictions were lifted and dealt with over 30,000 casework, policy, and campaign queries. I continue to dedicate myself to addressing key issues affecting our community. A Strong Local Voice One of my significant campaigns has been about the East West Rail project . I have consistently advocated for the interests and concerns of residents, pressing for the best outcomes regarding environmental impact and community benefits. I have opposed the plans in their current form, recognising that the consultation held by East West Rail was neither comprehensive nor transparent. I remain committed to keeping residents informed and engaged on this critical issue. Achieving Real Change on Matters that Concern You In May 2024, a major milestone was achieved with the passing of the Leasehold and Freehold Reform Act 2024. This legislation, which I championed following concerns raised with me by constituents, tackles unfair practices in freehold estate management charges , providing more rights and protections for homeowners. I also introduced the Hare Coursing Bill , targeting the cruel and illegal practice of hare coursing. This bill received strong support and underscores our commitment to animal welfare and rural crime prevention. The Government adopted my proposals in full, incorporating them into the Police Crime Sentencing and Courts Act 2022. Recognising the importance of sustainable development, I have been actively involved in discussions and actions concerning solar farm proposals in North Bedfordshire. My focus has been on balancing the need for renewable energy with the preservation of our beautiful countryside, ensuring that local voices are heard in planning processes. Addressing environmental concerns, I have worked on issues related to wastewater overflows and flooding . After meeting with affected constituents, I engaged with local authorities and stakeholders including the Mayor of Bedford Borough and the Leader of Central Bedfordshire to find solutions that protect our communities from pollution and flood risks. This led to proposing the formation of a North Bedfordshire Water Management Alliance, uniting Angl

pages.text_excerpt: Surgeries and Town Hall Meetings | Richard Fuller MP Skip to main content Main navigation The Economy About News Campaigns Surgeries Policy and Campaign Responses Contact Surgeries and Town Hall Meetings Tweet Richard holds regular advice surgeries for his constituents in North Bedfordshire. If you are not sure if Richard is your MP, please enter your postcode here . Surgeries are an opportunity for constituents to meet with Richard and bring their problems or concerns to his attention. Richard holds three types of surgery: In person, by appointment only Online or by telephone, by appointment only Drop-in, no appointment necessary To book a place at one of Richard’s in person or online/by telephone appointment-only surgeries, please call 01767 317224 or send an email to [email protected] . Please remember to include your full name, address and a contact telephone number and provide a brief outline of the issue you would like to raise with him. If your problem is urgent, Richard will always try to progress your case without an appointment and if you call the number above, Richard’s team is available to help. Please see below a list of Richard's upcoming surgeries: Friday 8 May 3-5pm - Appointment surgery. Great Denham Community Hall, 86 Saxon Way, MK40 4GP. Please call 01767 317224 to arrange an appointment. Friday 14 May 12-2pm - Online/by telephone. Please call 01767 317224 to arrange an appointment. Friday 22 May 12.30-2pm - Drop-in Surgery. Eyeworth Village Hall, High St, Eyeworth, SG19 2HH. No appointment necessary - please come along and share your views. Friday 12 June 3-5pm - Appointment surgery. Sandy Conservative Club, 19 Bedford Rd, Sandy SG19 1EL. Please call 01767 317224 to arrange an appointment. Richard also holds regular "town hall" meetings across the constituency. The next scheduled town hall meetings are set out below. These meetings are open to anyone in the constituency and provide an opportunity in a public forum to hear Richard's views on current issues and to ask him any questions: Thursday 7 May 7.30-9pm - Colmworth Village Hall, Church Road, Colmworth MK44 2JX News Richard held a town hall meeting in Bromham - next one in Colmworth on 7th May Monday, 27 April, 2026 Richard held his last public meeting for residents at Bromham Village Hall. Commenting afterwards he said:It was a fantastic turnout in Bromham for my latest ‘town hall’ meeting. Richard holds a drop-in surgery in Clapham - next one in Blunham on 24th April Wednesday, 15 April, 2026 Richard's last drop-in surgery took place at Clapham Village Hall. His next one is being held at Blunham Village Hall on Friday 24 April from 12.30-1.30pm. Richard holds a town hall meeting in Cardington - next one in Bromham on 16th April Wednesday, 8 April, 2026 Richard's last MP town hall meeting took place at the Village Hall and Reading Rooms in Cardington. Richard held a town hall meeting in Wyboston - next one in Cardington on Thursday 19 March Wednesday, 11 March, 2026 Richard's last 'town hall' meeting was held at Wyboston Village Hall. Richard held a drop-in surgery at Little Staughton - next one in Clapham on Friday 6 March Wednesday, 25 February, 2026 Last month, Richard held his drop-in surgery at Little Staughton Baptist Church.Speaking afterwards he said: I am grateful to those who came to see me at my recent drop-in surgery in Little Staughton.I was also very pleased to be able to sit down with Parish Councillor, Verity Hawkins, who kind Richard held a town hall meeting in Sandy - next one in Wyboston on Thursday 29 January Friday, 23 January, 2026 On Friday evening, Richard held his latest public meeting at Sandy Conservative Club. Richard held a drop-in surgery in Cotton End - next one is taking place in Little Staughton on Friday 30 January Friday, 23 January, 2026 Richard's last drop-in surgery took place at Cotton End Village Hall and his next once is being held from 12.30-2pm on Friday 30 January at Little Staughton Baptist Church.No appoint
